rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad area (Södermalm, Vasastan); SEK 12,000-18,000/mo; avg $1,499 reported; market tight, rents up 5-6% YoY into 2026

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; Swedish heating costs; internet ~SEK 350/mo

groceries.note: home cooking; ICA/Lidl/Coop; Sweden groceries moderate-high by European standards

diningOut.note: Stockholm dining expensive; lunch ~SEK 120-160, dinner SEK 200-400; lunch deals common

transit.note: SL monthly pass SEK 990 (~$95); excellent metro/bus network

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly from SEK 1,995 (~$190); good selection in Södermalm/Norrmalm

simData.note: SIM-only plan (Comviq/Hallon/Tele2); unlimited data ~SEK 200/mo; competitive market

misc.note: gym (~SEK 350), toiletries, entertainment, fika culture, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Stockholm medium-term furnished lets are stable year-round.

evidence.quote: a single person in Stockholm should plan on about $2,505 per month with rent, or $1,355 without housing
A 1-bedroom apartment in Stockholm costs $1,499.00/month on average; 1-bedroom rent ranges from SEK 12,000–18,000
The Stockholm rental market continues to face supply constraints, with rents rising roughly 5–6% year-over-year into 2026
